set.title: Homework Week 5 — Inequalities
set.description: Practice after covering AM-GM, Cauchy-Schwarz, and rearrangement.

problems.statement: If $a+b+c=6$ and $a,b,c\ge 0$, what is the maximum possible value of $abc$?
Which of the following is always true for positive reals $a, b$?
Prove that for all positive reals $a, b, c$ with $abc = 1$, $a^2 + b^2 + c^2 \ge a + b + c$.
problems.solutionSketch: Step 1: By AM-GM on $a^2, b^2, c^2$ and the constraint $abc = 1$, we have $a^2 + b^2 + c^2 \ge 3\sqrt[3]{a^2 b^2 c^2} = 3$. Step 2: By AM-GM on $a, b, c$ and $abc = 1$, we have $a + b + c \ge 3\sqrt[3]{abc} = 3$. Step 3: Combine with the power-mean inequality — specifically, since $a^2 + b^2 + c^2 \ge \frac{(a+b+c)^2}{3}$ (Cauchy-Schwarz / QM-AM), setting $s = a+b+c \ge 3$, it suffices to show $\frac{s^2}{3} \ge s$, i.e. $s \ge 3$, which we already have. Equality iff $a=b=c=1$.

problems.statement: Evaluate $\min_{x>0} \left(x + \frac{9}{x}\right)$. Give your answer as an integer.
Prove that for any real numbers $x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_n$, $\left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i\right)^2 \le n \sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i^2$.
problems.solutionSketch: Apply Cauchy-Schwarz to the vectors $(x_1, \ldots, x_n)$ and $(1, 1, \ldots, 1)$: $\left(\sum x_i \cdot 1\right)^2 \le \left(\sum x_i^2\right) \left(\sum 1^2\right) = n \sum x_i^2$. Equality iff all $x_i$ are equal.
